vocab.meaning: sự khác biệt, không nhất quán (giữa ảnh và thực tế)
vocab.example: There is often a slight discrepancy between the vibrant colors shown on the screen and the actual pair of shoes that arrives.

vocab.meaning: lấy khách hàng làm trung tâm
vocab.example: Most high-end footwear retailers have adopted a consumer-centric approach by offering free shipping and instant support.

vocab.meaning: sự hỗ trợ chỉnh hình (giúp bảo vệ chân)
vocab.example: When shopping online, I pay close attention to the technical descriptions to ensure the sneakers provide adequate orthopedic support.

vocab.meaning: thực tế ảo tăng cường (dùng để thử đồ ảo)
vocab.example: Some cutting-edge apps now allow you to use augmented reality to virtually try on shoes before committing to a purchase.
I only ever buy shoes from reputable websites that utilize a secure payment gateway to protect my financial data.
The biggest downside to buying designer boots online is the risk of accidentally purchasing counterfeit goods from unauthorized sellers.

vocab.meaning: kho hàng, danh mục hàng hóa
vocab.example: Online platforms boast a much more extensive inventory than local shops, featuring styles that aren't available elsewhere.

vocab.meaning: khuyến khích, thúc đẩy (bằng phần thưởng/ưu đãi)
vocab.example: E-commerce giants often incentivize customers to buy more by offering loyalty points or tiered discounts.

vocab.meaning: chứng chỉ/độ uy tín về tính bền vững (môi trường)
vocab.example: I prefer to order from brands that clearly state their sustainability credentials, such as using recycled ocean plastics for soles.

vocab.meaning: cơ sở hạ tầng hậu cần/vận chuyển
vocab.example: The rapid improvement in national logistics infrastructure means that online orders now reach my doorstep within 24 hours.
